# Items, Enemies & Star Chart
FORMAT: Resources/Fish/Gear/Quests: ## Name + description + drop sources. Enemies: ## Name with inline stats (Health | Shield | Armor | Faction) and drop tables. Star Chart: ## System with node tables (Node | Levels | Faction).
INDEX: Resources (599 entries) | Fish (47 entries) | Gear Items (179 entries) | Quests (45 entries) | Syndicates (28 entries) | Worldstate Reference Data (17 entries) | Enemies (479 entries) | Star Chart Nodes (22 entries)

# Resources
All crafting resources, their descriptions, and drop sources.

## 35mm Film
For the documentarian who sees the world through a unique lens, extra film is always a win. Trade this Hex Treasure with Lettie to earn standing with The Hex.
Location: 1999 (Höllvania)

### Acquisition
35mm Film can be found scattered across any Höllvania missions as an interactable pickup.
Farming Locations
Non-endless missions in Höllvania are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective.
Uses

Gathering Tips
As Hex Treasures (The Countessa Comic, On-lyne CD, Chuggin' Along Sixpack, Mood Crystal, Cheddar Crowns Cereal, 35mm Film, Argon Burger Meal and Big Bytes Pizza) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Höllvania tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
The surface of all Hex Treasures has a bright green glow to it, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

35mm Film is a Hex Treasure found within Höllvania. It can be exchanged for 750 Standing standing with The Hex. 1x 35mm Film is required to advance to the Rank 3 - 2-For-1 with the Hex and 1x 35mm Film is required to advance to Rank 5 - Pizza Party with the Hex.
**Type:** Resource

## Adramal Alloy
Purged of spores and contaminants, this alloy is highly versatile.
Blueprint sold by Otak in the Necralisk on Deimos.

### Acquisition
The reusable blueprint can be purchased from Otak for 1,000 Standing, requiring Rank 0 - Neutral with the Entrati.
Item Checklist for
38.0.12

### Wiki
In-game description
Adramal Alloy is a resource crafted from Adramalium. Each build yields 20 Adramal Alloy.
**Type:** Gem

## Adramalium
Sinewy and metallic, possessed of great elastic strength.
Location: Cambion Drift (Deimos) from Yellow Mining Lesions, Spitia Infested Cysts, and Rolizor Infested Cysts.

### Acquisition
Adramalium can be acquired from two different sources:
Mining yellow mineral veins in the Cambion Drift
Rolizor and Spitia Infested Cysts in the Cambion Drift
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1

### Wiki
In-game description
Adramalium is an ore that can be found in the Cambion Drift. It can be refined into Adramal Alloy.
**Notes:**
They can be traded at Otak in exchange for Otak Tokens in quantities of 7 to 25.
**Trivia:**
The name Adramalium might be referring to Adramelech, an Assyrian God and Christian demon, whose name probably translates to "Magnificent King".
**Type:** Gem

Sometimes a rig jockey needs a helping hand, so they get an advance on the next pay. Massive interest rates almost guarantee they'll be paying that off forever.
Obtained from Bounties in the Orb Vallis (Venus), or purchased from Ticker in Fortuna (Venus).

## Agnovidisc
A mark of identification carried by soldiers during The Old War so that their names might be remembered if they never returned home.
Location: Obtained from crates and enemies in Perita Rebellion missions (Dark Refractory).

### Wiki
In-game description
Agnovidisc is a common resource that can be found in The Perita Rebellion within the Dark Refractory. It can be acquired from enemies, lockers and loot containers.
Usage
Agnovidiscs are used for crafting Uriel, Vinquibus, Galariak Prime and Sagek Prime. They can also be exchanged for Marie Leroux's rotating offerings in La Cathédrale.
Item Checklist for
Warframes & Companions
Weapons
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/Recall: Dactolyst (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation A — Rare (3.00%)
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/Recall: Hunhullus (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation A — Rare (3.00%)
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/Recall: Vanguard (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation A — Rare (3.00%)
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/The Guilty (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation A — Rare (3.00%)

## Amarast
A violet gem that forms from the crystallization of byproducts expelled from the Weeping Towers.
Location: Orb Vallis (Venus) from Blue Mining Veins and Resource Containers

### Acquisition
Amarast can be acquired from two different sources:
Mined from Orb Vallis.
Dropped from storage containers found in Orb Vallis, yielding varying units.
Item Checklist for
Gathering Tips
As with all other rare gems, it is recommended to have a Resource Drop Chance Booster or a Resource Drop Chance Blessing active while mining this gem.
Amarast may be more common in blue veins near the Temple of Profit, in the far Northwestern corner of the map. Veins can be found in the nearby mushroom field and lake area.

### Wiki
In-game Description
Amarast is a resource that is obtainable by Mining the blue gem veins in Orb Vallis. Can be turned in to Solaris United's Standing by Smokefinger at Fortuna, Venus for 500 Standing each, or refined into Star Amarast.
**Trivia:**
Amarast is likely a portmanteau word based on amaranth, a purple-red flower, and amethyst, a purple quartz gem.
**Type:** Gem

## Argon Burger Meal
Burgers so tasty they always disappear!
Location: 1999 (Höllvania)

### Acquisition
Argon Burger Meals can be found scattered across any Höllvania missions as an interactable pickup.
Farming Locations
Non-endless missions in Höllvania are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective. alternatively, enemies do not start spwaning before activating kalymos in legacyte captures, allowing players to explore before completing any objectives and quickly capture the legacyte afterwards
Uses
Can be traded to Leticia for 1500 Standing with The Hex
Gathering Tips
As Hex Treasures (The Countessa Comic, On-lyne CD, Chuggin' Along Sixpack, Mood Crystal, Cheddar Crowns Cereal, 35mm Film, Argon Burger Meal and Big Bytes Pizza) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Höllvania tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
The surface of all Hex Treasures has a bright green glow to it, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

Argon Burger Meal is a Hex Treasure found within Höllvania. It can be exchanged for 1500 Standing standing with The Hex.
**Trivia:**
Its name and description are a reference to Argon Crystal.
The face on the box as well as the box's shape might be references to the McDonald's Happy Meal.
**Type:** Resource

## Ascaris Prime
Technology developed by Executor Ballas to allow remote piloting of a hijacked Warframe for short periods, forcing them to act against even their deepest instincts.
Obtained from Recall: Vanguard missions in The Perita Rebellion (The Dark Refractory).

### Acquisition
75 Ascaris Prime is awarded upon completion of The Old Peace quest.
Awarded from The Perita Rebellion's Recall: Vanguard:
Every Order completed in the Recall: Vanguard awards 1 Ascaris Prime.
Defeating the Prime Vanguard awards 10 Ascaris Prime.
In The Steel Path, defeating Commandeered Warframes (outside of the Prime Vanguard fight) may drop 2 Ascaris Prime, regardless of the Recall mission.
Not affected by Resource booster.
Can be bought from Marie Leroux in La Cathédrale, Sanctum Anatomica, in quantities of 15 in exchange for Agnovidisc, Laudavi, and/or Servoris. These purchases are limited to one per day, and are on a rotating basis.
Can be bought from the Market as part of the Tektolyst Artifact Acquisition Pack, in quantities of 150 for 175. This bundle can only be purchased once.
Usage
150 of each special resource from The Perita Rebellion missions (Lyroic Bridge, Ren Hypercore, and Ascaris Prime) is needed for each Focus School Tauron Weapon.
Each special resource from The Perita Rebellion is also used to upgrade the new Tauron Focus Nodes unlocked when you craft the weapon for each school, but the quantities differ per resource.
Obtaining and upgrading all 5 weapons requires 864 Lyroic Bridge, 912 Ren Hypercore, and 839 Ascaris Prime for a total of 2615 The Perita Rebellion resources.
Excluding the 750 of each resource it takes to obtain all 5 weapons, upgrading all 5 weapons to the max requires 114 Lyroic Bridge, 162 Ren Hypercore, and 89 Ascaris Prime for a total of 365 The Perita Rebellion resources.
Ascaris: The modern, non-Prime variant used by Captain Vor

### Wiki

Ascaris Prime is a special resource that can be obtained from the "Recall: Vanguard" mission in The Perita Rebellion.
**Type:** Resource | **Tradable**

### Drop Sources
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/Recall: Vanguard (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)

## Asterite
Formed over billions of years, these crystals are most often found embedded in space rock.
Location: Railjack Missions

### Wiki

Asterite is an uncommon resource that can be found in any Empyrean mission. It is used in upgrading the Railjack.
Item Checklist for
checklist
Gathering Tips
Destroying Asteroids, Red barrels, Metal scrap around wreckages, and Green rifts in any of the Empyrean missions has a chance to drop Asterite.
**Trivia:**
The name Asterite is derived from asteroid, an umbrella term for space rocks.
**Type:** Resource

## Atramentum
This deep black ink has the power to reveal hidden truth.
Obtained from Atramentum Balloons and completing Follie's Hunt missions (Vesper Relay). Exchanged with Aspirant Zorba in any Relay.

### Acquisition
After completing the Chains of Harrow quest, the Vesper Relay becomes a playable gamemode - Follie's Hunt.
Completing Follie's Hunt will reward 15 Atramentum (25 in The Steel Path).
During Follie's Hunt, 12 Atramentum Balloons will spawn in, with each dropping 2 - 4 Atramentum (3 - 6 in The Steel Path). Atramentum Balloons can only be destroyed with weapon damage; Atramentum Balloons are immune to ability damage.
Atramentum is not affected by Resource Booster, Resourceful Retriever, or Loyal Retriever.
Usage
Atramentum is primarily used to purchase Follie and Enkaus from Aspirant Zorba.
Item Checklist

### Wiki

thumb|right|295px|An Atramentum Balloon, located in Follie's Hunt.
Atramentum is a special resource obtained from the Follie's Hunt gamemode.
**Trivia:**
Atramentum or Atrament generally refers to a very black substance, usually liquid such as ink.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Venus/Vesper Relay (Follie's Hunt), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)
- Venus/Vesper Relay (Follie's Hunt), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)

## Aucrux Capacitors
Once installed, these delicate power systems fuse with a Corpus Obelisk's engines. Unused capacitors are therefore, highly prized.
Location: Railjack missions in Venus, Neptune, and Pluto Proximas

### Wiki

Aucrux Capacitors are a rare resource that can be found in any Empyrean mission. They are used in upgrading the Railjack.
Item Checklist for
checklist
There are no Starchart blueprints that requires.
38.0.12
**Trivia:**
Capacitors are a passive electronic component that store electrical energy, in contrast to the resource's description as a "power system".
**Type:** Resource

## Auron
A brilliant, dense and conductive metal.
Location: Plains of Eidolon (Earth) from Red Mining Veins and Thumpers

### Wiki
Auron is a resource obtainable by mining the red mineral veins in the Plains of Eidolon. Its main use is in crafting Auroxium Alloy.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1
Farming Tips
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the materials until better facts are proven.
Generally a rare metal to acquire, the cave entered through the base on top of Mount Nang possesses rich minable spots, including several occurances of Auron (Confirmed).
**Trivia:**
"Auron" seems to be little more than the Orokin (or perhaps Ostron) word for raw gold. It is derived from "aurum", the Latin word for gold. Gold is indeed a brilliant, dense (if soft) and conductive metal.
**Type:** Gem

### Drop Sources
- Narmer Thumper — Rare (16.68%)
- Tusk Thumper — Rare (16.68%)
- Narmer Thumper Bull — Rare (25.02%)
- Tusk Thumper Bull — Rare (25.02%)
- Narmer Thumper Doma — Rare (33.36%)
- Tusk Thumper Doma — Rare (33.36%)

## Bapholite
Malleable, owing to ingrown veins of Infestation protein.
Location: Cambion Drift (Deimos) from Yellow Mining Lesions, Rolizor Infested Cysts, and Bonus Vaults in Isolation Vault Bounties.

### Acquisition
Bapholite can be acquired from three different sources:
Mining yellow mineral veins in the Cambion Drift.
Storage containers found in the Bonus vaults found during Isolation Vault bounties.
Infested Rolizor Cysts on the Cambion Drift can house Bapholites within.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1

### Wiki
In-game description
Bapholite is an ore that can be found in the Cambion Drift. It can be refined into Tempered Bapholite.
**Notes:**
They can be traded at Otak in exchange for Otak Tokens in quantities of 7 to 25.
**Trivia:**
The name Bapholite might be derived from the name Baphomet, a pagan idol allegedly worshipped by the Knights Templar.
**Type:** Gem

Kuva has purchase limit of 7 times per week.

Beating Heartstrings is a rare component that can be obtained from Stage Defense, Solstice Square in Höllvania. It is used to purchase Temple and Riot-848 components from Flare, as well as other memorabilia.

## Bellow Voca
The deepest of Voidtongue moans, etched into stone. An unlikely asset in the construction of Albrecht's vessels.
Location: Albrecht's Laboratories (Deimos)

### Acquisition
Bellow Voca can be found scattered across any Albrecht's Laboratories mission as an interactable pickup.
Tier 3 and 4 Bounties from Fibonacci have a chance to award a Bellow Voca.
Every 8 hours, one Bellow Voca can be purchased from Loid using resources found in the Albrecht's Laboratories.
Farming Locations
Effervo
Non-endless missions in the Albrecht's Laboratories are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective. The Exterminate mission Nex is particularly effective as the player is naturally forced to maneuver a significant portion of the map.
Uses for Bellow Voca
38.5
Gathering Tips
As Voca (Shrill Voca, Bellow Voca and Echo Voca) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Albrecht's Laboratories tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
All Voca emit a low bellowing sound, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

Bellow Voca is an uncommon resource found within Albrecht's Laboratories on Deimos. They can be exchanged for 1000 Standing standing with the Cavia. 2x Bellow Voca are required to advance to the Rank 3 - Colleague and 10x for Rank 4 - Scholar with the Cavia.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Deimos/Albrecht's Laboratories (Level 75 - 80 Entrati Lab Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (12.40%)
- Deimos/Albrecht's Laboratories (Level 95 - 100 Entrati Lab Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (13.16%)

## Big Bytes Pizza
Who doesn't like pizza? Trade this Hex Treasure with Lettie to earn standing with The Hex.
Location: 1999 (Höllvania)

### Wiki
Zeke from On-Lyne
Big Bytes Pizza is a popular pizza chain in Höllvania.
1999 ARG
On the official site, near the bottom of the page where the three images are located, you can see a tomato sticking out from behind the rightmost image. Clicking on the tomato produces a sound, and clicking anywhere after that will throw and splatter a tomato centered on where you clicked. Doing so on the image of a pizza slice near the top of the menu (The one with five pepperoni slices on it) will reveal an image depicting the molecular structure Guanine (G), one of the four bases for DNA.
center|thumb|190x190px|Images located at the bottom of the Big Bytes Pizza website. Note the tomato sticking out from under the rightmost image.
center|thumb|The pizza image from the Big Bytes Pizza website. Splattered tomatoes reveal an image depicting the molecular structure for Guanine.
**Trivia:**
The official site includes the text and a phone number, "Call to order today! 1-800-555-2983". When this number is dialed on the phone in the site for the City Wide Metro, it will play a jingle from the song "Tarantella Napoletana", which is associated with Italy and pizza in popular media.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Höllvania (Level 105 - 110 WF1999 Bounty), Rotation C — Rare (4.00%)

## Calda Toroid
A device of undetermined function and origin. Prized by Vox Solaris and the Quills.
Earn Calda Toroids from enemies located at the Enrichment Labs in the Orb Vallis and from Scyto Raknoids. They can also be found as pick-ups in caves located around the Orb Vallis (Venus).

### Wiki
In-game Description
The Calda Toroid is a rare resource that can be acquired in caves or as a rare drop from enemies near the Enrichment Labs in Orb Vallis or any Scyto Raknoid. This resource acts either as a normal pickup when dropped by enemies, or as an interactive object if found in caves.
Redeeming Calda Toroid will award 1,000 Standing with Vox Solaris. One is also used as a sacrifice for Rank 1 - Operative and Rank 3 - Hand. It is also used as a crafting component for the Garuda and Baruuk Warframes, as well as various Operator suit, Amp parts and Hound (Companion) parts.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1
Gathering Tips
Go to Enrichment Labs. Try to get one of the enemies to put down a Reinforcement Beacon and wait until the beacon hits Alert Level 4. From this point, unlimited enemies will spawn.
Commonly used Warframes for farming Toroids are Khora's Pilfering Strangledome, Hydroid's Pilfering Swarm, or Nekros' (Desecrate).
**Notes:**
thumb|Caves with Toroid spawnsCalda Toroids may be found in every cave with a Toroid spawning area. The chance to find this kind of Toroid seems to be higher around the Enrichment Labs area.
It is possible to find several Toroids at once in a cave. Those Toroids may also be of different kinds.
30.9
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 40 - 60 PROFIT-TAKER - PHASE 2), Rotation C — Uncommon (12.50%)
- Orb Vallis - Enrichment Labs Enemies — Uncommon (2.00%)
- Narmer Scyto Raknoid — Common (20.00%)
- Scyto Raknoid — Common (20.00%)

## Carbides
Resilient components used to reinforce the plated sheets of metal strapped to Grineer Ships.
Location: Eximus units in the Grineer Shipyard Tileset, and Railjack missions in Earth, Saturn, and Veil Proximas

### Wiki

Carbides are an uncommon Resource dropped by Eximus units found in the Grineer Shipyard tileset, primarily Ceres and Sedna, and can also be found in Empyrean missions as a possible loot drop. They are predominantly used in constructing, upgrading, and maintaining the Railjack.
Carbides are also dropped Earth Proxima missions by Grineer, Storage Containers and ships.
Item Checklist for
checklist
40.0.5.1
Gathering Tips
Carbides are significantly more common in Empyrean than in the Grineer Shipyard tileset.
Kuva Lich and Arbitrations missions can spawn Eximus units more often as a result of their higher level, allowing for easier farming.
Any Endless mission types on Ceres is a great source for Eximus units, with higher waves spawning more Eximus units.
Seimeni, Ceres is a Dark Sector Defense mission that is very good for farming, yielding 100 or more Carbides at wave 20 due to high Eximus spawn rate.
The Mobile Defense node Kiste on Ceres can be good for a quick way to farm due to the high chance of Prosecutors spawning. These Prosecutors are considered as Eximus units and can drop the resource.
Carbides can be farmed in Sorties on Lua with Eximus Stronghold condition.
**Notes:**
Carbides can be found on Berehynia and Marid, Sedna, which use the Grineer Shipyard tileset.
**Trivia:**
A carbide is a compound of carbon and a metal. As per the resource's description, some real-life carbides are incredibly durable and very heat-resistant compared to other metals.
This resource is presumably used for riveting.
1pLjo1fWxyw
**Type:** Resource

## Cephalite Resonance
Cephalite Resonance used to enter the Glassmaker's Weave

### Wiki
In-game Description
Cephalite Resonance is a unique resource that drops during Nightwave Series 3. It has a chance to drop from Cepahlites: enemies that have been vitrified by Glass Fissures. These fissure can randomly spawn during missions, similar to how Void Fissures corrupt enemies.
Five of these are needed to access the Glassmaker's Weave in each Glassmaker crime scene.
**Notes:**
Nora Night will alert the player as the Glass Fissures spawn.
Fissures can also spawn in open-world areas such as the Plains of Eidolon and Orb Vallis and the on-foot sections of the Empyrean game mode.
Glass Fissures do not spawn during Void Fissures.
Most Bosses, including Kuva Liches, cannot be vitrified.
Breaking the glass clusters covering Cephalite enemies will cause them to become vulnerable.
Cephalites will explode shortly after death, dealing Slash damage to all units within range (both Tenno and enemies).
Resonace drops are not affected by Resource Boosters or Resource Drop Chance Boosters.
However, they are affected by loot abilities (Desecrate, Prowl, Retrieve) and Warframe Augment Mods (Ore Gaze, Pilfering Swarm, Pilfering Strangledome).
5 Cephalite Resonance is consumed instantly when used to investigate the energy resonance in the crime scene.
The player cannot carry more than 20 Cephalite Resonances.
Tip
During Railjack missions, Resonance can be easily farmed without running around, as Glass Fissures will always turn enemies boarding the Railjack, given that there is no point of interest for ground enemies to spawn.
Void Blast can easily destroy glass clusteers on Cephalites, without aiming for each specific part.
Similarly, most Melee attacks can easily destroy the glass clusters very quickly, requiring little to no aiming on your part.
**Type:** Resource

## Cerulean Fungus Extract
Extracted from a Cerulean Fungus colony.
**Type:** Plant

## Cetus Wisp
Most common at night, these stone lifeforms are drawn to shorelines touched by the Eidolon. They linger around lakes on the Plains of Eidolon at all hours. Their origin remains mysterious.
Location: Plains of Eidolon on Earth

### Wiki

Cetus Wisp are odd beings found hovering on the edge of most bodies of water in the Plains of Eidolon, and are also purchasable from The Quills for 2,000 Standing (available at Rank 5 - Architect), or during Operation: Plague Star from Operational Supply for 750 Standing + 1,500.
Behavior
Cetus Wisp are generally found on the banks of Gara Toht Lake and the smaller pools of landlocked water. They will be deposited in the player's inventory should the player manage to touch it.
Identified more as a loot pickup than a non-hostile NPC, they are widely used as a resource for crafting. Wisps are typically used as a component in Amp and Zaw components, but are also used in Gara's chassis and Revenant's systems.
Item Checklist
40.0.5.1
**Tips:**
thumb|right|Cetus Wisp Spawn Locations (incomplete)
thumb|right|Cetus Wisp optimal farming route (optimised using genetic algorithm)
Cetus Wisp spawn locations are shared between players. They can be picked up and marked by other players.
Wisps show up as loot on the Plains. Therefore, Loot Detector, Animal Instinct, and Thief's Wit can be used to identify them, greatly reducing time used for searching.
Wisps are not highlighted by Codex or Synthesis Scanners.
Vacuum and Greedy Pull will not pick them up and players must manually collect the Wisp, given that Wisps also ignores the 3-meter universal vacuum.
Wisps can be picked up while in Archwing. Itzal allows players to quickly skim fly around using Cosmic Crush ability to suck up wisps. Bring a Loot Detector or a companion with Animal Instinct to see them in the Archwing (Thief's Wit won't work).
Wisps can be picked up by Titania while in Razorwing form simply by flying through the wisp.
Wisps can be picked up by the Chesa Kubrow.
Wisps can be encountered at any time of day, though they can be harder to see at night. The blue shimmer on the ground at night in fact makes these harder to see.
**Type:** Resource

## Cheddar Crowns Cereal
A nostalgic treat for the ultimate '90s kid! Remember those commercials with the cheerful mouse quartet? Trade this Hex Treasure with Lettie to earn standing with The Hex.
Location: 1999 (Höllvania)

### Acquisition
Cheddar Crowns Cereal boxes can be found scattered across any Höllvania missions as an interactable pickup.
Tier 5 Bounties from the Bounty Board have a chance to award Cheddar Crowns Cereal.
Farming Locations
Non-endless missions in Höllvania are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective.
Uses

Gathering Tips
As Hex Treasures (The Countessa Comic, On-lyne CD, Chuggin' Along Sixpack, Mood Crystal, Cheddar Crowns Cereal, 35mm Film, Argon Burger Meal and Big Bytes Pizza) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Höllvania tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
The surface of all Hex Treasures has a bright green glow to it, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

Cheddar Crowns Cereal is a Hex Treasure found within Höllvania. It can be exchanged for 750 Standing standing with The Hex. 1x Cheddar Crowns Cereal is required to advance to the Rank 3 - 2-For-1 with the Hex and 1x Cheddar Crowns Cereal is required to advance to Rank 5 - Pizza Party with the Hex.
**Trivia:**
The back of the box lists the "Calories", "Cheese", and "Uranium" content: "Yes", "Yes", and "Probably Not".
It also states they are licensed as "Cheese Crowns" by "Cheese Group Limited", and that the batch number is "476577098" "706986996".
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Höllvania (Level 95 - 100 WF1999 Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (11.76%)

The surface of all Hex Treasures has a bright green glow to it, making them easier to locate.

## Copernics
A cell of encapsulated Void energy.
Location: Lua

### Wiki

Copernics were a common Resource dropped by Eximus units found in the Orokin Moon tileset, primarily Lua. They could have also been found in Empyrean missions as a possible loot drop. They were used in constructing, upgrading, and maintaining the Railjack.
Blueprints Requiring Copernics

29.9.1
Gathering Tips
Copernics are significantly more common in Empyrean than in the Orokin Moon tileset.
Kuva Lich and Arbitrations missions can spawn Eximus units more often as a result of their higher level, allowing for easier farming.
Any Endless mission types on Lua is a great source for Eximus units, with higher waves spawning more Eximus units.
Copernics may be dropped as an end mission reward of Railjack missions at amounts of 1200, 1500 or 2700 depending on luck. (Empyrean Rewards)
**Notes:**
When running Empyrean missions to farm Copernics, all Copernics, Carbides, Cubic Diodes, and Pustrels are stored in the Railjack's Resource Forge in order to manufacture consumable items. The Forge holds a default of 200 of each resource, after which additional gathering is automatically distributed to the squad, until the mission is completed. Pressing the "Refine" button from one of the Forge station will distribute the resources to the entire squad.
Although being a Void tileset resource, Copernics seem to have a very low drop chance, even lower than Argon Crystals. It is advised to use resource boosters while farming them outside of Railjack missions.
**Type:** Resource

## Coprun
A soft, malleable metal.
Location: Plains of Eidolon (Earth) from Red Mining Veins

### Wiki
In-Game DescriptionCoprun is a common resource acquired through mining red ore veins on the Plains of Eidolon. Its main use is in the creation of Coprite Alloy.
Item Checklist for
**Trivia:**
Based on its appearance and description as soft and malleable, Coprun appears to be the Orokin or Ostron name for raw copper. The name is based in the Latin word for copper, which is "cuprum".
**Type:** Gem

Corpus Cipher is a special component which could only be received as rewards in special alerts during the Operation: Arid Fear. It is one of two components required to create a Corpus Void Key.

Unlike normal alerts one could repeat the Arid Fear alerts until the time ran out meaning one could do multiple runs on the same alert.

## Crisma Toroid
A device of undetermined function and origin. Prized by Vox Solaris and the Quills.
Earn Crisma Toroids by defeating the Profit-Taker Orb on the Orb Vallis (Venus).

### Wiki
In-game Description
The Crisma Toroid is a resource that drops after defeating the Profit-Taker Orb during Phase 4 Heist in Orb Vallis.
Redeeming Crisma Toroid will award 6,000 Standing with Vox Solaris. One Crisma Toroid is also used as a sacrifice for Rank 5 - Shadow. It is also used in the research and crafting of Larkspur.
Item Checklist for
General
Research
38.0.12
**Trivia:**
The name likely derives from "crimson" for the toroid's reddish color, and is in line with the naming of the Lazulite Toroid.
The term "crisma" also has several meanings in various languages. Mostly, the meanings are associated with Christianity.
**Type:** Resource

## Cubic Diodes
Vital components used in the creation of Corpus photon-networks.
Location: Eximus units in the Corpus Ice Planet Tileset, and Railjack missions in Venus, Neptune, and Pluto Proximas

### Wiki

Cubic Diodes are an uncommon resource that can be found from Eximus units on the Corpus Ice Planet tileset, primarily Europa. They can also be found in Empyrean missions as a possible loot drop. They are used in constructing, upgrading, and maintaining the Railjack.
Item Checklist for
checklist
40.0.5.1
Gathering Tips
Cubic Diodes are significantly more common in Empyrean than in the Corpus Ice Planet tileset.
Kuva Lich and Arbitrations missions can spawn Eximus units more often as a result of their higher level, allowing for easier farming.
Any Endless mission types on Europa is a great source for Eximus units, with higher waves spawning more Eximus units.
Ose, Europa is an Interception mission that can yield a moderate amount of Cubic Diodes.
Larzac, Europa is a Dark Sectors Defense mission that is very good for farming, yielding 100 or more at wave 20 due to high Eximus spawn rate.
Nereid, Neptune is a Spy mission with the Corpus Ice Planet tileset. Eximus units often spawn if a player triggers the alarms in a vault.
Syndicate Death squads spawned on the Corpus Ice Planet tileset can drop Cubic Diodes because they all spawn as Eximus units.
Cubic Diodes may be dropped as an end mission reward of Empyrean missions as the extra resource bundle. Drop locations vary based on the Proxima Region. See Empyrean#Rewards.
**Notes:**
Cubic Diodes can be found on Nereid and Neso, Neptune, which use the Corpus Ice Planet tileset.
Marduk, Void is a Sabotage mission using the Corpus Ice Planet tileset when fighting the Lynx. Players may also get Cubic Diodes if Eximus units spawn in this tileset or in the Orokin Tower tileset during that mission.
Trigering alarms in Spy vaults spawns Eximus units, allowing for consistent drops.
**Type:** Resource

Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1

Dagonic is a gem that can be found in the Cambion Drift. It can be refined into Purged Dagonic.
**Notes:**
They can be traded at Otak in exchange for Otak Tokens in quantities of 4 to 25.
**Trivia:**
The name Dagonic is derived from Dagon, an ancient Mesopotamian and Canaanite deity associated with agriculture and fish.

## Dusklight Sarracenia
This robust, carnivorous plant has adapted to conditions in polluted waters.
Location: Obtained by gathering Dusklight Sarracenia plants growing in Grineer Shipyards found commonly on Ceres

### Wiki
This robust, carnivorous plant has adapted to conditions in polluted waters.
Dusklight Sarracenias are Plants found only in Grineer Shipyards, which are most commonly found on Ceres within pools of toxic waste.
Usage Checklist for Dusklight Sarracenia
38.0.12
Farming Locations
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resources until better facts are proven.
Ker
Gathering Tips
Oxylus' Botanist will automatically loot plants within range.
Pallas, Ceres will always spawn two of these in the green water next to spawn.
Ker, Ceres has a guaranteed large reactor room that will spawn around 4 of these.
**Trivia:**
Sarracenia is a genus of Pitcher plants commonly known as Trumpet Pitchers, and are endemic to the North American continent. It is unknown if the Dusklight Sarracenia has any biological relation to the Lunar Pitcher.
Amaryn's hint about the location of this flower mentions it grows in "100% filth", which she concludes by wondering what kind of flower grows in those conditions. This is a likely reference to darkSector, wherein a character describes the Lotus flower as a "rare beauty [that] blooms among filth and decay".
**Type:** Plant

## Echo Voca
The quietest and most elusive of Voidtongue utterances. Sought after but never found by Albrecht.
Location: Albrecht's Laboratories (Deimos)

### Acquisition
Echo Voca can be found scattered across any Albrecht's Laboratories mission as an interactable pickup.
Killing Gruzzlings have a chance to leave Echo Voca pickups.
Tier 5 Bounties from Fibonacci have a chance to award an Echo Voca.
Every 8 hours, one Echo Voca can be purchased from Loid using resources found in the Albrecht's Laboratories.
Uses for Echo Voca
Farming Locations
Effervo
Non-endless missions in the Albrecht's Laboratories are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective. The Exterminate mission Nex is particularly effective as the player is naturally forced to maneuver a significant portion of the map.
Gathering Tips
As Voca (Shrill Voca, Bellow Voca and Echo Voca) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Albrecht's Laboratories tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
All Voca emit a low bellowing sound, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

Echo Voca is a rare resource found within Albrecht's Laboratories on Deimos. They can be exchanged for 2000 Standing standing with the Cavia. 1x Echo Voca are required to advance to the Rank 4 - Scholar and 10x for Rank 5 - Illuminate with the Cavia. It is also an important crafting material for Qorvex, Grimoire and the Helminth Coalescent Segment.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Deimos/Albrecht's Laboratories (Level 115 - 120 Entrati Lab Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (14.71%)

## Efervon Sample
A sample of the corrosive substance that Scaldra use to purge Techrot. Handle with care.
Location: 1999 (Höllvania)

### Wiki
In-game description
Efervon Samples are a common resource found in Höllvania. They can be acquired from enemies, loot containers, and Efervon Sample Stockpiles.
Overview
Efervon is a hazardous chemical agent used by the Scaldra to purge the Techrot from Höllvania. It is hazardous to organic life, and is employed by the Scaldra to fumigate and purge infected districts. Even the Scaldra troopers themselves aren't immune to its effects, as they can be heard wheezing and having a raspy voice as they speak.
Liquid Efervon can often be found in large storage tanks around the city's streets. Damaging the spouts will cause the tanks to rupture, leading to the Efervon within spilling out and forming a large corrosive puddle that can damage both nearby players and enemies, inflicting Corrosive and Gas damage.
Item Checklist for
38.5
Farming Locations
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resource until better facts are proven.
**Trivia:**
Efervon appears to be a reference to Enferon, a gas that is highly lethal to creatures infected by the Technocyte Virus, in Digital Extremes' earlier title Dark Sector.
Major Neci Rusalka wears a gas mask that allows her to inhale an Efervon pipe like cigarette, which is revealed in Isleweaver fragments to be her combating her lethal Techrot infection in her lungs.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Höllvania (Level 105 - 110 WF1999 Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (14.00%)
- Höllvania (Level 55 - 60 WF1999 Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (14.63%)

## Entrati Lanthorn
A ceremonial beacon used to map Void locations on to real space.
Location: Albrecht's Laboratories (Deimos) and Zariman

### Acquisition
Guaranteed drop from Netracell runs. Will still drop after the limit of 5 weekly runs is completed.
Killing Gruzzlings has a chance to leave a pickup that yields three Entrati Lanthorn.
Can be obtained from some Sanctum Anatomica endless missions as rotation rewards: Alchemy, Mirror Defense and Survival, with a 5% chance to award one each on the B rotation.
Can be awarded from Zariman Ten Zero bounties and mission rewards.
As a Region Resource for the Zariman Ten Zero:
Rarely found from Storage Containers in the Zariman.
Randomly awarded to its owner by Smeeta Kavat with Charm in any Zariman mission.
Randomly extractable by Extractor deployable in the Zariman system from Star Chart.
Rare drop from any enemy killed on the Zariman.

checklist
38.5
Farming Locations
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resource until better facts are proven.
The Greenway
Gathering Tips
Guaranteed drop from Netracell runs.
Chroma's Elemental Ward, Limbo's Cataclysm, and Xaku's The Vast Untime modded for maximum range are effective at destroying storage containers. It is also advisable to bring weapons that deal AoE damage.
Extractors have a chance to yield Entrati Lanthorns.

### Wiki

Entrati Lanthorn is a rare resource that can be found in the Zariman Ten Zero and Albrecht's Laboratories.
**Trivia:**
Lanthorn is an archaic form of the word lantern.
Entrati Lanthorn is named after the Entrati, probably to honor Albrecht Entrati as the first person who successfully accessed the Void, or because it was invented by the Entrati.
The Entrati Lanthorn's model resembles an uncorrupted version of the Concoction used to access Isolation Vaults. While this could just simply be a reused model, this may indicate what the Concoctions were originally used for before the Infested overwhelmed Deimos.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources

Blueprints Requiring Fresnels

29.9.1
Gathering Tips
Since this item can be found in any area, it would be best to farm it like any other resource. It has been suggested to go to Posit Cluster on Earth Proxima, which would be the easiest mission.
**Trivia:**
The name and description of this resource refers to the Fresnel lens, a special lens used primarily in lighthouses and theaters for the purpose of bending light into a straight beam, without using too much glass during production.

## Gorgaricus Spore
The reproductive spore of the giant Vallis fungi is highly coveted for the complex chemical compounds it contains.
Location: Orb Vallis on Venus

### Wiki
In-game Description
Gorgaricus Spore is resource that can be found all over Orb Vallis, being purple sacs that can be found hanging beneath the caps of the giant mushrooms. Mature Gorgaricus Sacs are purple in contrast to the immature, orange sacs growing with them, and are harvested by attacking the sacs and letting them fall to the ground. Gorgaricus Spores are also rewarded from Bounties, or from resource containers all over the Vallis.
Item Checklist for
General

The Gorgaricus' name is a portmanteau of gorge and Agaricus, a genus of mushrooms that includes the widely cultivated button mushroom.

## Grandmother Token
A rare sign of Grandmother's favor.
Obtained from Grandmother in the Necralisk on Deimos.

### Acquisition
There are currently 3 ways of obtaining Grandmother Tokens:
Trading other Family member tokens with Grandmother. The Grandmother Token is always worth 50% more than the other tokens used to trade for it.
Finding them in the Cambion Drift.
Trading random assortment of Cambion Drift resources under the Mend the Family option with Grandmother.
Use
Grandmother Tokens can be traded with Grandmother for 3000 Standing Entrati Standing.
Other Family Tokens can also be traded for 100 Standing (Mother Token), 500 Standing (Father Token, Daughter Token, Otak Token), or 1000 Standing (Son Token).
10 Grandmother Tokens can be traded with Grandmother for 1 Seriglass Shard, a component used in weapon crafting. Also 1 Seriglass Shard is required as a sacrifice to attain Family rank with the Entrati.
Can be traded with Grandmother for different Decorations.

### Wiki
In-game description
A Grandmother Token is one of the Entrati Family Tokens.
**Type:** Resource

## Granum Crown
An ancient token of esteem from the Corpus Founder himself.

### Wiki
Granum Crown are a form of currency prized by the Corpus, said to be tokens of esteem from their founder Parvos Granum. Carried by Treasurers on Corpus Ships, the Tenno can defeat these Treasurers to claim the Crowns for their own use.
Granum Crowns will only appear after finishing The Deadlock Protocol quest.
Mechanics
In missions in the Corpus Ship tileset, Treasurers will spawn within 2-4 minutes in non-endless missions and within 5-8 minutes in endless missions.
The Treasurer will announce his presence with dialogue and a waypoint, and players must kill the Treasurer within a short timeframe to obtain a Granum Crown.
Note that only one Treasurer can spawn in a mission, and thus only one Crown can be acquired per mission.
The Crowns are unaffected by Vacuum or Fetch, and must be manually picked up by players.
There are three tiers of Granum Crowns, each of which are found in a given level range. The Crowns' level ranges, as well as their descriptions, are listed below:
The Treasurer does not spawn in Valac, Europa due to it being a Crossfire mission, despite using the Corpus Ship tileset.
Uses
There are three uses for Granum Crown - all within the Corpus Ship tileset:
accessing the Granum Void
freeing captive Solaris
gaining rewards from the Corpus Temple Reliefs
Note that players can spend Crowns acquired in a mission within the same mission.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Exemplar Granum Crown Cache — Common (100.00%)
- Granum Crown Cache — Common (100.00%)
- Zenith Granum Crown Cache — Common (100.00%)
- Granum Void, Rotation A — Uncommon (16.67%)

## Heciphron
Aggregates in the neo-lymph systems of Infested masses.
Location: Cambion Drift (Deimos) from Blue Mining Lesions.

### Wiki
In-game description
Heciphron is a gem that can be found in the Cambion Drift. It can be refined into Purified Heciphron.
Heciphron can be acquired by mining blue mineral veins in the Cambion Drift.
Heciphron is most commonly found within Albrecht's Prospect, initiating a Tier 2 Isolation bounty is recommended to get to more mining nodes within the area.
The caves within the Catabolic Gutter provide a large quantity of mining nodes and a solid spread of rarities.
Hitting the bonus zone while mining is helpful for acquiring Heciphron.
Heciphron can also be acquired from doing Requiem Obelisks in the Cambion Drift.
Killing enemies with Operator correctly under the Requiem Obelisks effect.
Umbra kills while in Operator form counts as Operator kill.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1
**Notes:**
Heciphron can be traded at Otak in exchange for Otak Tokens in quantities of 4 to 25.
**Trivia:**
The name is probably derived from the Greek term "Hekaton" meaning "Hundred" and the suffix "-phron" meaning "Mind" or "Intellect", roughly translating to "Hundred-Minded", possibly referencing the hive minded nature of the Infested.
**Type:** Gem

## Hesperon
Clay that strengthens into extremely durable stone when brought to temperatures below zero.
Location: Orb Vallis (Venus) from Red Mining Veins and Resource Containers

### Wiki
In-game Description
Hesperon is a resource obtainable by Mining the red mineral veins in Orb Vallis. Its main use is in crafting Hespazym Alloy. It can also drop from any container in Orb Vallis.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1
Farming Tips
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the materials proven.
The drop-chance for tier 3 (rare) materials increases substantially depending on the distance between the ore and the open-world spawn location (i.e., Fortuna).
Many high-density mining spots in Orb Vallis are not as applicable to Hesperon mining, since they are located relatively close to Fortuna.
A sample Hesperon run is shown on the right, with points of interest in red, and outdoor regions in orange.
thumb|A sample hesperon ore mining route. Higher density caves are marked in red, while outdoor routes to be scanned are shown orange.
Other regions and tips for finding red Hesperon veins:
The lake and the mushroom forest in the northwest at the Temple of Profit.
The southwest corner around the Asta Crater.
The southwest of the Temple Fabrication (due to the relatively high number of mineral deposits in the area).
The Deck 12 Cave contains a large, consistent cluster of red mineral veins and typically has few to no enemies. Exiting and re-entering Deck 12 usually respawns the mineral veins as well.
Steel Path is NOT reccomend for farming this resource, due to it reducing the amount of red veins (increasing blue).
**Trivia:**
This resource's name is derived from Hesperus, the personification of the planet Venus in Greek mythology.
Hesperon's description calls into mind permafrost, soil whose temperature is below 0°C (or 32°F) for at least two years. Frozen permafrost is quite hard, though contrary to its name it is capable of thawing.
**Type:** Gem

## Iradite
A mineral valuable to the Ostrons.
Location: Plains of Eidolon on Earth

### Wiki
A rich vein of Iradite.

Iradite is a resource that can be found on low-elevated areas on the Plains of Eidolon, which includes Twin Horns, Seaside Ruin, Hek's Stiletto, Er-Phryah's Vigil, Renthi Spring and The Seethe. They appear in deposits that can be destroyed with attacks, with the amount of Iradite being tied to the area's level range. Iradite is also a reward from Bounties.
Item Checklist
40.0.5.1
Gathering Tips
Beginning a level 40-60 bounty will cause deposits to yield 3-5 Iradite. Bounty stages need to be completed to benefit.
Using Limbo's Cataclysm or Xaku's The Vast Untime with maximum range and high efficiency will break containers and deposits.
Killing Tusk Thumpers in the Plains of Eidolon is a possible way to farm Iradite in batches.
Because the amount gained scales based on enemy level, Steel Path instances will yield ~5-10 Iradite per node. Invisibility can be used to gather significant quantities of Iradite quickly in this way.
If you start the Narmer bounty you'll have a guaranteed 5 Iradite from every node
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Iradite Formation — Common (100.00%)
- Ostron (Old Man Suumbaat), Surah — Common (100.00%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 5 - 15 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (15.18%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 5 - 15 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (15.49%)
- Narmer Thumper — Rare (16.68%)
- Tusk Thumper — Rare (16.68%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 5 - 15 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (20.00%)
- Narmer Thumper Bull — Rare (25.02%)
- Tusk Thumper Bull — Rare (25.02%)

## Kesslers
Orokin devices used for navigating the solar winds.

### Wiki

Kesslers were an uncommon resource that could be found in any Empyrean mission. They were used in researches and upgrading the Railjack. They seemed to drop most commonly around Saturn Proxima.
Blueprints Requiring Kesslers

29.9.1
**Trivia:**
The name Kesslers is probably a reference to Donald J. Kessler, an American astrophysicist and former NASA scientist known for his studies regarding space debris. In 1978 he coined the term Kessler syndrome, a theoretical scenario in which the density of objects in low Earth orbit is so high that collisions between them could result in even more space debris, fuelling additional collisions and making future space activities near-impossible.
**Type:** Resource

## Lua Thrax Plasm
A unique variant of Thrax Plasm harvested during Void Conjunctions on Lua.
Location: Yuvarium and Circulus on Lua

### Wiki

Lua Thrax Plasm is a rare resource found in Conjunction Survival.
Access to this resource requires completion of The War Within quest.
Guaranteed Rotation reward from Conjunction Survival.
Dropped by Hollow Thrax Centurion, Eclipse Thrax Centurion, Hollow Thrax Legatus, and Lua Thrax Legatus that appear after each Rotation in Conjunction Survival at a 30% chance.
Item Checklist
Usage
Archimedean Yonta#Trade for Lua Thrax Plasm
Gathering Tips
Lua Thrax Plasm dropped by Centurions are affected by Resource Booster.
Plasm given as a rotation reward are not affected by Resource Boosters.
As number of Thrax Centurions spawned at the end of each rotation is number of members in the squad plus 1, doing a mission in a team has a potential to get more Lua Thrax Plasms than doing solo.
Plasm drops will despawn after 60 seconds, so make sure to grab them quickly.
**Notes:**
To purchase all Lua Thrax Plasm offerings from Yonta at least once, players will need 1,110 Lua Thrax Plasm.
Purchasing a fully-ranked copy of one Arcane Enhancement will require an additional 315.
Purchasing one fully-ranked copy of each Arcane will require an additional 1,260.
to acquire one of everything coupled with the crafting costs, players will need a total of 2,595.
Including a second copy of Voruna for Helminth, players will need a total of 2,720 Lua Thrax Plasm.
**Type:** Resource | **Tradable**

### Drop Sources
- Lua/Circulus (Survival), Rotation A — Common (100.00%)
- Lua/Circulus (Survival), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)
- Lua/Circulus (Survival), Rotation C — Common (100.00%)
- Lua/Yuvarium (Survival), Rotation A — Common (100.00%)
- Lua/Yuvarium (Survival), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)
- Lua/Yuvarium (Survival), Rotation C — Common (100.00%)

Oxylus' Botanist will automatically loot plants within range.
**Trivia:**
The plant's codex description references how Pitcher plants prey on insects (and in some cases, shrews) by luring them into its "pitcher", whose inner surface is slippery, which causes any unfortunate animal to fall into a pool of digestive juices and drown. It is unknown if the Lunar Pitcher has any biological relation to the Dusklight Sarracenia.
Two overgrown Lunar Pitchers can be found in the Helminth Room.
There can sometimes spawn a Lunar Pitcher in the extraction tile that is only barely scannable since the terrain almost completely covers it. Two small portions of it are scannable from two different angles.
**Type:** Plant

## Lyroic Bridge
A delicate paraneural interface allowing a duly evolved Sentient pilot to command the colossal Hunhullus as if it were a second skin.
Obtained from Recall: Hunhullus missions in The Perita Rebellion (The Dark Refractory)

### Acquisition
75 Lyroic Bridge is awarded upon completion of The Old Peace quest.
Awarded from The Perita Rebellion's Recall: Hunhullus:
Every Order completed in the Recall: Hunhullus awards 1 Lyroic Bridge.
Defeating the Hunhullus awards 10 Lyroic Bridge.
In The Steel Path, defeating Commandeered Warframes (outside of the Prime Vanguard fight) may drop 2 Lyroic Bridge, regardless of the Recall mission.
Not affected by Resource booster.
Can be bought from Marie Leroux in La Cathédrale, Sanctum Anatomica, in quantities of 15 in exchange for Agnovidisc, Laudavi, and/or Servoris. These purchases are limited to one per day, and are on a rotating basis.
Can be bought from the Market as part of the Tektolyst Artifact Acquisition Pack, in quantities of 150 for 175. This bundle can only be purchased once.
10 Lyroic Bridge can be obtainted from the mission "The Guilty" (Hardmode Boss) in The Perita Rebellion, as a repeated mission clearing reward.
Usage
150 of each special resource from The Perita Rebellion missions (Lyroic Bridge, Ren Hypercore, and Ascaris Prime) are needed for each Focus School Tauron Weapon.
Each special resource from The Perita Rebellion is also used to upgrade Tauron Focus Nodes unlocked when you obtain the weapon for each school, but the quantities differ per resource.
Obtaining and upgrading all 5 weapons requires 864 Lyroic Bridge, 912 Ren Hypercore, and 839 Ascaris Prime for a total of 2615 The Perita Rebellion resources.
Excluding the 750 of each resource it takes to obtain all 5 weapons, upgrading all 5 weapons to the max requires 114 Lyroic Bridge, 162 Ren Hypercore, and 89 Ascaris Prime for a total of 365 The Perita Rebellion resources.

### Wiki

Lyroic Bridge is a special resource that can be obtained from the "Recall: Hunhullus" mission in The Perita Rebellion.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/Recall: Hunhullus (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/The Guilty (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)

## Maphica
A core of rage, jealously guarded in a burnished shell.
Location: Obtained from crates and bosses in the Descendia (Dark Refractory).

### Acquisition
The Descendia:
Potentially awarded in quantities of 5 on Infernums 2, 4, 9, 11, 16, and 18.
Potentially awarded in quantities of 15 on Infernums 2, 9, and 16 on The Steel Path.
Dropped from Storage Containers.
Drops are not affected by Resource Booster or Loyal Retriever/Resourceful Retriever.
Can be bought from Marie Leroux in La Cathédrale, Sanctum Anatomica, in quantities of 10 in exchange for Agnovidisc, Laudavi, and/or Servoris. These purchases are limited to one per day.
Usage
Maphica can be used to purchase Uriel, Vinquibus, Galariak Prime, and Sagek Prime blueprints and components, 4 Warframe, 3 Primary, 1 Secondary, and 1 Melee Arcanes, the Insign Bundle, and 8 Captura scenes as an alternative to Dark Refractory drops; only the Insign Bundle and Captura scenes are exclusive to Roathe's wares.

## Mood Crystal
Sleek, stylish, and soothing, mood crystals are the perfect gift for anyone in need of a little calm. Trade this Hex Treasure with Lettie to earn standing with The Hex.
Location: 1999 (Höllvania)

### Acquisition
Mood Crystals can be found scattered across any Höllvania missions as a Hex Treasure and make the associated noises.
Tier 4 Bounties from the Bounty Board have a chance to award a Mood Crystal.
Farming Locations
Non-endless missions in Höllvania are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective.
Uses

Gathering Tips
As Hex Treasures (The Countessa Comic, On-lyne CD, Chuggin' Along Sixpack, Mood Crystal, Cheddar Crowns Cereal, 35mm Film, Argon Burger Meal and Big Bytes Pizza) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Höllvania tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
The surface of all Hex Treasures has a bright green glow to it, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

Mood Crystal is a Hex Treasure found within Höllvania. It can be exchanged for 750 Standing standing with The Hex. 1x Mood Crystal is required to advance to Rank 3 - 2-For-1 with The Hex and 1x Mood Crystal is required to advance to Rank 5 - Pizza Party with The Hex.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Höllvania (Level 85 - 90 WF1999 Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (11.11%)

## Moonlight Dragonlily
This uncommon plant can only be found at night.
Location: Obtained by gathering Moonlight Dragonlily plants that grow on Earth

### Wiki
This uncommon plant can only be found at night.
Moonlight Dragonlilies are Plants found only in Earth's Grineer Forests during nighttime, often growing near grassy areas or bodies of water.
Usage Checklist for Moonlight Dragonlily
38.0.12
Farming Locations
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resources until better facts are proven. Note that the Moonlight Dragonlily can only spawn when it is nighttime on the Grineer Forest tileset.
Cervantes
Gathering Tips
Oxylus' Botanist will automatically loot plants within range.
A good mission to look into is Cervantes, Earth or E Prime, Earth on the lowermost levels of the maps around the air burst vents can yield 4-5 scans possible without boosters.
**Trivia:**
The Dragonlily's name is derived from one of the Dracunculus vulgaris' nicknames. Its appearance is based on blossomed Liliums, a genus of herbaceous flowering plants growing from bulbs that are considered true lilies.
On Lith, Earth, two Moonlight Dragonlillies always spawn in the same place. From the starting area, hug the left border of the map and keep progressing to find the first one. The second one can be found just past the first one, in a tunnel by hugging the left border.
**Type:** Plant

## Mother Token
A mark of Mother's esteem.
Obtained by completing Bounties in the Cambion Drift on Deimos.

### Acquisition
There are currently 3 ways of obtaining Mother Tokens:
Completing Bounties given out by Mother.
Finding them in the Cambion Drift.
Trading random assortment of Cambion Drift resources under the Mend the Family option with Grandmother.
Use
Mother Tokens, as well as other Family Tokens, can be traded with Grandmother for 100 Standing Entrati Standing. They can also be traded into Grandmother Tokens.
Mother Tokens are additionally used to purchase event-exclusive items from the yearly Nights of Naberus event.

In-game description
A Mother Token is one of the Entrati Family Tokens.
**Type:** Resource

## Mutagen Sample
These samples could advance our knowledge of biological research.
Location: Eris and Deimos

### Wiki

Mutagen Sample is a resource that can be obtained from enemies and containers on Deimos and Eris. It is usually found in quantities of 1.
Mutagen Samples are used for Bio Lab Research & Blueprints from the Clan Dojo.
Item Checklist for
General
Research
40.0.5.1
Farming Locations
Xini
**Notes:**
Mutagen Sample cannot be bought for Platinum at the Market.
**Tips:**
Since Mutagen Sample are considered a rare drop on Eris while being uncommon on Deimos, it is advised to visit the latter when attempting to farm Mutagen Samples efficiently.
**Trivia:**
Mutagen Sample had several changes to their drop locations:
From Update 8 to Update 10, they could drop from Eris and Jupiter, which at the time were controlled by the Infested.
From Update 10 to Update 14.1.0, they were limited to the Orokin Derelict, which became Deimos in Update 29.
From Update 14.1.0 onwards Eris could drop Mutagen Samples once more.
25.7.6

## Mytocardia Spore
A fungal body prized for its varied uses.
Location: Orb Vallis on Venus

### Wiki
In-game Description
Mytocardia Spore is resource that can be found in Mytocardia Sacs strewn all over Orb Vallis, dropping 1-3 Spores per Sac. growing beneath giant mushrooms on the ground. Mytocardia Spores are also rewarded by Bounties.
The mushroom forest to the West of the Coolant Reservoir, and the area around the lake South of Transit Depot are particularly rich locations, and the area around the SPACEPORT
Playing higher level bounties does not appear to increase how many Mytocardia Spores are dropped by each Sac.
Item Checklist for
General
Research
40.0.5.1
**Trivia:**
Mytocardia is a portmanteau of the prefix myco-, which refers to fungi, and the word cardia, which refers to cardiac muscle and alludes to the spore's resemblance to a human heart.
Mysteriously the Mytocardia Sac has two Codex entries with only varying by the depicted sac in two slightly different views.

## Orokin Ballistics Matrix
A mech matrix that assists with the machine's weapon accuracy and control.
Location: Isolation Vault Bounty rewards on the Cambion Drift (Deimos), and from Enemy Necramechs found in Tier 2 Isolation Vault Bounties

### Acquisition
Orokin Ballistics Matrices are an uncommon reward in the Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 Isolation Vault bounties offered by Mother. They are rewarded in quantities of x2, x3, and x4 respectively.
Guaranteed to drop from killing Necramechs guarding the Tier 2 Isolation Vaults.
Resource Booster and loot abilities such as Nekros' Desecrate increase the amount of Orokin Matrices acquired from killed Necramechs.
Use
Orokin Ballistics Matrices can be turned in to Loid in the Necralisk for 2,500 Standing Necraloid Standing each.
15 are needed for ranking up to Clearance: Modus (rank 2) with Necraloid.

## Otak Token
Otak is grateful for your aid. This helps him to remember it.
Obtained from Otak in the Necralisk on Deimos.

### Acquisition
There are currently 3 ways of obtaining Otak Tokens:
Trading Gems and Ores from the Cambion Drift with Otak.
Finding them in the Cambion Drift.
Trading random assortment of Cambion Drift resources under the Mend the Family option with Grandmother.
Use
Otak Tokens, as well as other Family Tokens can be traded with Grandmother for 500 Standing Entrati Standing. They can also be traded into Grandmother Tokens.

### Wiki
In-game description
An Otak Token is one of the Entrati Family Tokens.
**Type:** Resource

## Pathos Clamp
The manifestation of Thrax's malevolence that shapes the Orowyrms' outbursts.
Location: Rewarded upon defeating the Orowyrm in Duviri

### Acquisition
Looted from the chest reward after defeating an Orowyrm, the final boss in each Duviri cycle, in quantities of 10 (15 in The Steel Path).
Looted from the chest reward in Isleweaver after defeating the Duet boss fight against The Fragmented, in quantities of 10 (15 in The Steel Path).
Clearing The Undercroft side portals in The Duviri Experience, in quantities of 1 (3 in The Steel Path).
The amount dropped from these sources is not affected by Resource Boosters.
Usage
Pathos Clamp can be used to purchase the Drifter melee weapons bundled together with their Warframe-compatible blueprints from Teshin's Cave. Syam, Azothane and Edun can be purchased for 50 Pathos Clamp, while the Argo & Vel and Sampotes can be purchased for 60, totaling to 270 Pathos Clamps.
Acrithis, found in both the Dormizone and Duviri, also sells Orokin Reactors, Orokin Catalysts, Exilus Warframe Adapter, Exilus Weapon Adapter, Primary Arcane Adapter, Secondary Arcane Adapter, Forma Blueprint, Veiled Riven Mods, and Kuva for 10-25 Pathos Clamp.
Item Checklist for
Pathos Clamp are required when installing an Incarnon Genesis Adapter.
38.0.12

### Wiki
Pathos Clamp is a special resource from Duviri acquired from defeating Orowyrms.
**Notes:**
To acquire all current Drifter Melee weapons, Incarnon Genesis upgrades of every type, Kullervo, and Rauta, players will need 1680 Pathos Clamp. Totaling at least 168 (112 on Steel Path) Orowyrm kills.
Not affected by Resource Booster.
**Trivia:**
Pathos Clamp appear to be lumps of flesh taken from an Orowyrm.
Pathos is the Greek word (πάθος) for passion.
**Type:** Resource

## Phasmin

## Plantitem
**Type:** Plant

## Pustrels
A parasitic growth that often infects Eximus units operating in Infested environs.
Location: Eris

### Wiki

Pustrels were a common Resource dropped by Eximus units found in the Infested Ship tileset, primarily on Eris. They could also be found in Empyrean missions as a possible loot drop from destroying Grineer ships, killing Grineer ground units, or opening storage containers found on Crewships. They were used in constructing, upgrading, and maintaining the Railjack.
Blueprints Requiring Pustrels

29.9.1
Gathering Tips
Pustrels were significantly more common in Empyrean than in the Infested Ship tileset.
Kuva Lich and Arbitrations missions can spawn Eximus units more often as a result of their higher level, allowing for easier farming.
Any Endless mission types on Eris was a great source for Eximus units, with higher waves spawning more Eximus units.
Pustrels could have dropped as an end mission reward of Railjack missions at amounts of 1200, 1500 or 2700. (Empyrean Rewards)
**Notes:**
When running Empyrean missions to farm Pustrels, all Pustrels, Carbides, Cubic Diodes, and Copernics were stored in the Railjack's Resource Forge in order to manufacture consumable items. The Forge holds a default of 200 of each resource, after which additional gathering is automatically distributed to the squad, until the mission is completed. Pressing the "Refine" button from one of the Forge station will distribute the resources to the entire squad.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 28, Pustrels could be found through Mining on the Plains of Eidolon. Despite this, they would not drop from any Tusk Thumper.
With their design clashing with the more natural look of ores and gems, Pustrels are most likely the result of the Infested boil that crashed into the Plains in Operation: Plague Star.
Update 27 introduced the ability to get Pustrels (and some other resources) through Empyrean missions in the form of loot drops and successful mission rewards.
**Type:** Resource

## Pyrol
A tarnished, brittle metal.
Location: Plains of Eidolon (Earth) from Red Mining Veins and Thumpers

### Wiki

Pyrol is obtainable by mining red mineral veins on the Plains of Eidolon. It can also drop from Tusk Thumpers. It is mainly used in crafting Pyrotic Alloy and Tempered Bapholite.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1
**Notes:**
40 Pyrol is required to achieve Visitor rank with the Ostron.
**Trivia:**
It is possible that Pyrol is the Orokin or Ostron name for the mineral pyrite, also known as "fool's gold". Pyrite is a brittle iron sulfide with the chemical formula FeS2.

## Pyrus Essence
With a blazing orange hue and rigid material strength, this compound is prized by ship and station architects.

### Acquisition
Pyrus Essence can be gained on either Earth, Mercury, Ceres, or Saturn by knocking down and capturing Pyrus Essence Carrier, Eximus-like units that drop Pyrus Essence upon being captured.
The amount of Pyrus Essence gained per capture depends on the planet, with Essence Carriers on Earth, Mercury, Ceres, and Saturn dropping 1, 2, 3, and 4 Pyrus Essence respectively.
Players are required to obtain a total of 30 Pyrus Essence in the event.

### Wiki
In-game description
Pyrus Essence is a material used for the Relay Construction event, beginning on August 30th, 2018 on PC, October 4 on Xbox One and PS4, and August 7th, 2019 on Nintendo Switch.
During the event, and after the event ends, this can be sold for 10.
**Type:** Resource

## Ren Hypercore
The evolved core of a Dactolyst, bearing its true self-given name and identity. When deprived of Tau pollen, the core becomes susceptible to forcible reprogramming.
Obtained from Recall: Dactolyst missions in The Perita Rebellion (The Dark Refractory).

### Acquisition
75 Ren Hypercore is awarded upon completion of The Old Peace quest.
Awarded from The Perita Rebellion's Recall: Dactolyst:
Every Order completed in the Recall: Dactolyst awards 1 Ren Hypercore.
Defeating the Dactolyst awards 10 Ren Hypercore.
In The Steel Path, defeating Commandeered Warframes (outside of the Prime Vanguard fight) may drop 2 Ren Hypercore, regardless of the Recall mission.
Not affected by Resource booster.
Can be bought from Marie Leroux in La Cathédrale, Sanctum Anatomica, in quantities of 15 in exchange for Agnovidisc, Laudavi, and/or Servoris. These purchases are limited to one per day, and are on a rotating basis.
Can be bought from the Market as part of the Tektolyst Artifact Acquisition Pack, in quantities of 150 for 175. This bundle can only be purchased once.
Usage
150 of each special resource from The Perita Rebellion missions (Lyroic Bridge, Ren Hypercore, and Ascaris Prime) are needed for each Focus School Tauron Weapon.
Each special resource from The Perita Rebellion is also used to upgrade the new Tauron Focus Nodes unlocked when you craft the weapon for each school, but the quantities differ per resource.
Obtaining and upgrading all 5 weapons requires 864 Lyroic Bridge, 912 Ren Hypercore, and 839 Ascaris Prime for a total of 2615 The Perita Rebellion resources.
Excluding the 750 of each resource it takes to obtain all 5 weapons, upgrading all 5 weapons to the max requires 114 Lyroic Bridge, 162 Ren Hypercore, and 89 Ascaris Prime for a total of 365 The Perita Rebellion resources.

### Wiki

Ren Hypercore is a special resource that can be obtained from the "Recall: Dactolyst" mission in The Perita Rebellion.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Dark Refractory, Deimos/Recall: Dactolyst (The Perita Rebellion), Rotation B — Common (100.00%)

## Riven Transmuter
Combine multiple Rivens to create a new, different Riven.

### Acquisition
Two transmuters are guaranteed to drop upon capturing an Eidolon Hydrolyst in the Plains of Eidolon at night. Killing the Hydrolyst will not drop Riven transmuters.
Can be purchased for 180 Vessel Capillaries from Loid in Sanctum Anatomica.
Can be purchased for 10 Riven Slivers from Palladino in Iron Wake on Earth with a purchase limit of 3 per week, requires completion of Chains of Harrow.

### Wiki
Riven Transmuter is an item used in Riven Mod Transmutation. It allows players to sacrifice four Rivens in their inventory for a new, veiled Riven mod. Upon transmutation, the four selected Riven mods and the Riven Transmuter will be consumed. The new Riven will always be of the same type as one of the transmuted Rivens. As a consequence, if four of the same type are transmuted (e.g. only rifle Rivens), then the new mod will be of the same weapon class.
**Tips:**
While the Riven Transmuter cannot be traded, it is possible to trade 4 Riven Mods, have them transmuted then trade back the veiled mod.
__forcetoc__
**Type:** Resource

### Acquisition

Item Checklist for
Images

### Wiki
In-game description
Rune Marrow is a resource from Duviri.
**Tips:**
Seven to eleven containers spawn per Undercroft tile.
Missions like Defense or Excavation will start when players move to the objective, starting the mission can be delayed to allow an efficient squad to quickly search the tile in peace for maximum gain.
As companions (except for Khora's Venari) cannot be brought into Duviri, mods like Loot Detector or Thief's Wit on the Warframe are recommended.

## Scintillant
These scarce, hovering relics of the Orokin era can still be found monitoring the isolation vaults deep beneath the surface of Deimos.
Location: Cambion Drift on Deimos

### Acquisition
Scintillant can be obtained in the following ways:
Rare Reward from Tier 1 Bounties from Mother, and a common reward from Tier 2 Bounties.
Drops from hostile Necramechs found during Isolation Vault Bounties.
Found in Isolation Vaults as floating entities that can be picked up.
Scintillant entities have a chance to respawn.
Item Checklist for
40.0.5.1
Gathering Tips
Thief's Wit, Loot Detector, and Animal Instinct will display Scintillant on the mini map.
Scintillant are floating around, so having a high loot radar and watching the minimap for moving loot can be an indicator for Scintillant.
Scintillant produce a unique sound that can be heard from a short distance.
Scintillant found floating around in the Isolation Vault underground are affected by Resource Boosters.

## Sentirum
A rare Eidolon Gem, created from the fossilized blood of an ancient Sentient.
Location: Plains of Eidolon (Earth) from Blue Mining Veins and Thumper Bulls. This Rare Gem can only be mined by the Advanced Nosam Cutter or Sunpoint Plasma Drill.

### Wiki

Sentirum is a gem acquired through mining in the Plains of Eidolon that can be acquired from blue veins. Additionally, successfully hitting the special narrow brackets during mining will grant additional gem rewards on completion, even on red ore veins.
It can be refined to make Radian Sentirum or traded for Ostron faction standing at Old Man Suumbaat for 400 Standing each.
The Advanced Nosam Cutter or the Sunpoint Plasma Drill is required to mine this gem. The Cutters have a 7.5% and 10% chance respectively to give it from blue mining nodes or bonus brackets on any mining node.
Item Checklist for
**Notes:**
On death, the Tusk Thumper Bull and Tusk Thumper Doma in the Plains of Eidolon may drop Sentirum.
1x Sentirum is required to achieve Kin with the Ostrons.
**Type:** Gem

### Drop Sources
- Operational Supply, Neutral — Common (100.00%)
- Narmer Thumper Bull — Uncommon (25.00%)
- Tusk Thumper Bull — Uncommon (25.00%)
- Narmer Thumper Doma — Uncommon (50.00%)
- Tusk Thumper Doma — Uncommon (50.00%)

## Seriglass Shard
Once part of an intricate Entrati apparatus, now shattered and bearing a sharp edge.
Location: Grandmother in the Necralisk on Deimos

### Acquisition
Seriglass Shards can be purchased from Grandmother in the Necralisk for 10 Grandmother Tokens each.
Lore
Necraloid
The Seriglass Shards appear to be the shattered pieces of Albrecht Entrati's Seriglass Bell, which shattered into fragments upon his first voyage into the Void.
This is supported by the item's description stating it was once part of a larger apparatus.
Upon waking on the floor of his laboratory, Albrecht himself used one of the broken pieces to write "Close it" (referring to the portal he had just opened) in his own blood.
It seems that his heirs saved the pieces of the Seriglass Bell, as they can be obtained by the player from Grandmother, and Mother is mentioned by Father as keeping a shard for herself (and even using it as a weapon against him).
Item Checklist for

### Wiki
In-game description
A Seriglass Shard is a resource that can be purchased from Grandmother in the Necralisk and is used to craft Entrati weapons. It is also required as a sacrifice to attain Family rank with the Entrati.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Entrati (Grandmother) — Common (100.00%)

## Servoris
Medals awarded to Orokin soldiers during The Old War for acts of distinguished service.
Location: Obtained from crates and enemies in Perita Rebellion missions (Dark Refractory).

### Wiki
In-game description
Servoris is a rare resource that can be found in the The Perita Rebellion within the Dark Refractory. It can be acquired from enemies, lockers and loot containers.
Usage
Servoris are used for crafting Uriel, Vinquibus, Galariak Prime and Sagek Prime. They can also be exchanged for Marie Leroux's rotating offerings in La Cathédrale.

## Shrill Voca
A Voidtongue cry, trapped in the stone. Crucial to unraveling the secrets of the Murmur.
Location: Albrecht's Laboratories (Deimos)

### Acquisition
Shrill Voca can be found scattered across any Albrecht's Laboratories mission as an interactable pickup.
Tier 1 and 2 Bounties from Fibonacci have a chance to award a Shrill Voca.
Every 8 hours, one Shrill Voca can be purchased from Loid using resources found in the Albrecht's Laboratories.
Farming Locations
Effervo
Non-endless missions in the Albrecht's Laboratories are recommended since players can explore the tileset after completing the objective. The Exterminate mission Nex is particularly effective as the player is naturally forced to maneuver a significant portion of the map.
Uses for Shrill Voca
38.5
Gathering Tips
As Voca (Shrill Voca, Bellow Voca and Echo Voca) are located as Syndicate-like medallions all around the Albrecht's Laboratories tileset, having a Warframe and/or companion with large loot detection capability (Loot Detector, Thief's Wit, Animal Instinct), using the Orokin Eye Air Support and infusing Golden Instinct will greatly help in recovering these items.
All Voca emit a low bellowing sound, making them easier to locate.

### Wiki

Shrill Voca is a common resource found within Albrecht's Laboratories on Deimos. They can be exchanged for 500 Standing standing with the Cavia. 3x Shrill Voca are required to advance to the Rank 1 - Assistant, 5x for Rank 2 - Researcher and 8x for Rank 3 - Colleague with the Cavia. It is also an important crafting material for Ekhein.
**Type:** Resource

### Drop Sources
- Deimos/Albrecht's Laboratories (Level 55 - 60 Entrati Lab Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (13.04%)
- Deimos/Albrecht's Laboratories (Level 65 - 70 Entrati Lab Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (13.56%)
- Deimos/Armatus (Disruption), Rotation A — Rare (9.09%)

## Silphsela
It is traditionally held that to see Silphsela floating in the air heralds an imminent change in the sky.
Location: Duviri

### Acquisition
Silphsela are acquired from Sun Silphs that are typically found on hill slopes within Duviri. They have 4 seeds floating in the air around them, and 3 more burst when approaching one from 8 meters away.
Silphsela may also sometimes be obtained from Teshin's Cave.
Gathering Tips
Many Silphsela flowers can be found within the snowy region of Duviri typically near Titan's Rest.
They can appear as tiny fire-like objects during the Anger Spiral in the sky, even without a nearby Sun Silph. The easiest way to collect these are by riding the Kaithe.
Item Checklist for

### Wiki
Silphsela is a resource in Duviri.
**Type:** Resource
